title: Data Retention Standard
document_text: Klavora Technologies Data Retention Standard. Effective date: 2026-01-15. Review date: 2027-01-15. This standard applies to engineering, security, finance, and people operations teams that manage production systems or employee records. The Security Director owns the standard. System logs must be retained for 180 days. Payroll records must be retained for seven years. Customer support tickets containing account actions must be retained for two years. Teams must delete expired records within 30 days of the retention period ending unless a legal hold is active. Exceptions require written approval from the Security Director and Legal Counsel. Failure to follow this standard may result in audit findings, suspension of system access, or disciplinary action.

key_obligations.obligation: Retain payroll records for seven years.
Delete expired records within 30 days after the retention period ends unless a legal hold is active.

exceptions: Written approval from the Security Director and Legal Counsel is required for exceptions.
A legal hold pauses deletion requirements.
required_actions: Apply retention schedules to covered record categories.
Delete expired records within 30 days unless a legal hold applies.
Escalate exception requests for written approval.
risk_flags: Legal hold handling can delay deletion.
Multiple teams own different record classes, so coordination failures are possible.

summaries.employee_summary: Covered teams must keep logs, payroll records, and support-ticket records for the stated retention periods and delete records after they expire unless a legal hold applies. If you need an exception, obtain written approval from the Security Director and Legal Counsel.
summaries.ops_summary: The standard sets dated retention controls across operational and HR-related record classes, with the Security Director as primary owner. Control risk is concentrated in deletion timing, cross-team ownership, and exception approvals, so audit evidence should show schedules, deletion execution, and legal-hold tracking.

title: Remote Access Policy
document_text: Klavora Technologies Remote Access Policy. Effective date: 2026-02-01. Review date: 2027-02-01. This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and interns who access company systems outside a Klavora office. The IT Operations Manager owns implementation, and the Security Director approves exceptions. Users must connect through the approved VPN, use company-managed devices, enable multi-factor authentication, and lock devices when unattended. Shared credentials are prohibited. High-risk vendor access must be reviewed quarterly. Violations may lead to access revocation, incident investigation, and disciplinary action up to contract termination.

key_obligations.obligation: Connect through the approved VPN when accessing company systems remotely.
Use company-managed devices and enable multi-factor authentication.

violations_or_consequences: Disciplinary action up to contract termination
required_actions: Use the approved VPN for remote access.
risk_flags: Remote access from unmanaged devices is disallowed.
High-risk vendor access requires recurring review.

summaries.employee_summary: If you work remotely, use the approved VPN, a company-managed device, and MFA every time you access company systems. Do not share credentials, and expect access to be revoked if the policy is violated.
summaries.ops_summary: The policy puts IT Operations on implementation and exception control with Security approval. Main control points are VPN enforcement, device management, MFA coverage, and a quarterly review cycle for high-risk vendor access.

title: Expense Reimbursement Procedure
document_text: Klavora Technologies Expense Reimbursement Procedure. Effective date: 2026-01-05. Review date: 2026-12-15. This procedure applies to all full-time employees and department managers. The Finance Operations Lead is responsible for administration. Employees must submit itemized receipts and expense reports within 10 business days of the purchase. Managers must approve eligible expenses within 5 business days of submission. Travel expenses above 1500 USD require pre-approval from the department director. Exceptions are allowed only for emergency travel and must be documented in the expense system. Late or unsupported claims may be rejected and repeated non-compliance may affect reimbursement privileges.

key_obligations.obligation: Submit itemized receipts and expense reports within 10 business days of purchase.
Approve eligible expenses within 5 business days of submission.
Obtain pre-approval for travel expenses above 1500 USD.

exceptions: Emergency travel can be processed as an exception if documented in the expense system.
violations_or_consequences: Repeated non-compliance may affect reimbursement privileges.
required_actions: Submit receipts and reports within the deadline.
Document emergency-travel exceptions in the expense system.
risk_flags: Late submissions can cause reimbursement disputes.

summaries.employee_summary: Submit receipts and expense reports within 10 business days, and get pre-approval before any travel expense above 1500 USD. Emergency travel is the only stated exception, and it must be documented in the expense system.
summaries.ops_summary: Finance Operations owns the procedure, but manager approval timeliness is a key dependency. The highest operational risk areas are overdue submissions, undocumented emergency exceptions, and missing pre-approval for high-value travel.
